notes: SW-123 (EVAL-02) budgets for the hero-task suite and the pinned real-repo runs, FROZEN per ADR 0003 U5 from the first reproducible run on the reference runner class (see recorded_from). Ratchet policy (bench/bench-budget.yml precedent): budget ~= 2x the measured latency baseline (shared-runner variance headroom), ~1.5x the memory/size baseline, rounded to round numbers. Re-pinning is a reviewed edit of this file citing a newer reference run - never a code change. The gate fails when a measured value EXCEEDS its budget.

hero_suite.notes: The reference run measured <1ms (millisecond-granularity floor) for every hero task on the tier-1 fixtures, so per-op millisecond budgets carry no ratchet signal there; the hero ratchet is the 20/20 pass gate (cmd/eval/hero_test.go + the eval-full hero job). Latency ratchets live in real_repos.warm_p95_us, where microsecond resolution supports them.

real_repos.notes: guava peak_rss baseline nearly fills the 16GB reference runner; the ADR 0003 U4 disposition (whole-graph cache off the stable path) is expected to lower it - re-pin DOWNWARD after that story lands. agent_tools p95 is dominated by agent_brief's full-catalog digest (ADR 0003 U2): cobra 8.5ms / flask 5.2ms / guava 184ms while every other op stays <= 1.3ms.
